title: Commercial Strategy Manager | Product (all genders)
required_skills: 5+ years in software pricing or monetisation strategy at B2B enterprise SaaS
Experience pricing AI-powered products or multi-solution platform offerings
Strong analytical skills with pricing research methodologies
Proven track record translating insights into commercially validated pricing decisions
Experience working within a Product organisation
bonus_skills: Experience with enterprise financial software, CPM, or regulatory reporting solutions
MBA or advanced degree in economics, business, or quantitative discipline
matched_skills: Cross-functional stakeholder management: demonstrated at HSBC (Product, Engineering, Marketing, C-suite) and Hapag-Lloyd
Analytical skills and data storytelling: structured insight reports, executive dashboards
German B1 (partial — JD requires fluent German)
missing_skills: Software pricing or monetisation strategy (no SaaS pricing experience at all)
AI-powered product pricing (no product pricing background)
Pricing research methodologies (willingness-to-pay interviews, structured pilots, win/loss analysis)
B2B enterprise SaaS experience (no SaaS company background)
Product organisation experience (candidate is PMO/coordination, not product strategy)
core_responsibilities: Design and own pricing architecture for multi-solution platform including value-metric frameworks and AI-capability tiering
Conduct annual pricing reviews and market research (competitor pricing, willingness-to-pay, segmentation)
Validate pricing hypotheses through structured pilots, regional rollouts, and win/loss analysis
Co-own commercial readiness for new product launches with GTM leadership
Build business cases with scenario modelling to quantify revenue impact
Organise internal pricing review sessions for Executive Leadership Team
recommended_emphasis: This role is a poor match — application is not recommended
If applying anyway, highlight IPC GTM strategy and 4.6M CNY contract contribution as closest commercial evidence
Emphasise ECE Group competitive analysis and market intelligence work as partial proxy for pricing research
Acknowledge the core gaps (SaaS pricing, product org, German fluency) honestly — do not overstate credentials
summary: Match score 18.2% — poor match. The role requires deep SaaS pricing and monetisation expertise, AI product pricing experience, and fluent German, none of which the candidate possesses. The candidate's background is in PMO coordination and operational management, not commercial product strategy in a SaaS context. Application is not recommended; the skills gap is fundamental and not bridgeable through reframing.
